# CLAUDE.md

Guidance for working in this repository. This is the **GrowthBook CLI**, a command-line tool
**generated by [Speakeasy](https://speakeasy.com) from the GrowthBook OpenAPI spec**. It is a
Go CLI (entrypoint `./cmd/growthbook`) distributed as a standalone binary.

## The golden rule: don't hand-edit generated code

Most files here are generated (`cmd/`, `internal/sdk/`, the per-resource command packages, etc.
— look for the `// Code generated by Speakeasy ... DO NOT EDIT.` header). Editing them directly
is overwritten on the next `speakeasy run`.

**To change the CLI, change the inputs, not the output:**
- **The OpenAPI spec is the source of truth.** It's generated upstream in the `growthbook`
  back-end from Zod validators (`pnpm --filter back-end generate-openapi`). Genuine API/spec
  bugs (wrong types, missing fields, invalid path params) should be fixed **there** and the
  spec regenerated.
- **`.speakeasy/overlay.yaml`** — vendor-specific, codegen-compatibility tweaks that should
  *not* pollute the public spec (e.g. `x-speakeasy-enums`, `x-speakeasy-group`). The overlay
  re-applies on every generation, so fixes here survive the auto-update pipeline.
- **`.speakeasy/gen.yaml` / `.speakeasy/workflow.yaml`** — generation config and the spec
  source/output wiring.

The overlay currently carries deliberate fixes (enum identifier collisions, a scalar
`version` path parameter, and a command-group rename to dodge a reserved package name). Each
action is commented — **don't remove them without understanding why**, or generation/compilation
will break.

## Build & regenerate

```bash
speakeasy run                                   # regenerate from spec + overlay
go mod tidy                                     # if go.sum is incomplete after a regen
go build -o growthbook ./cmd/growthbook         # build the CLI
./growthbook --help
go build ./...                                  # quick check the whole module compiles
```

**Go must be installed wherever generation runs — including CI.** Without it, `speakeasy run`
skips the compile and `go mod tidy` steps, so `go.sum` is left incomplete and the output won't
build. (Don't commit a generated tree that hasn't been compiled.)

## Working with Speakeasy (skill: `speakeasy-context`)

- Run **`speakeasy agent context`** before a Speakeasy task; **`speakeasy agent feedback -m`**
  after. Prefer the CLI's own context/`--help` over external docs.
- **`persistentEdits`**: keep customization in inputs (overlay/`gen.yaml`), not hand-edits to
  generated files — answer **No** to the preservation prompt unless we have intentionally
  added hand-written Go (then use `persistentEdits` or `.speakeasy/patches/`).

## Gotchas (learned the hard way)

- **Overlay JSONPath is finicky.** Only recursive equality filters reliably match:
  `$..[?(@.field=="value")]` (double quotes). Array-index predicates (`@.enum[1]==...`),
  nested-existence (`@.schema.anyOf`), regex (`=~`), and array-filter-after-key
  (`$.paths..parameters[?()]`) **silently match nothing**. Always verify a target before
  trusting it:
  ```bash
  speakeasy overlay validate -o .speakeasy/overlay.yaml
  speakeasy overlay apply -s <spec> -o .speakeasy/overlay.yaml | grep <your-marker>
  ```
- **`speakeasy lint` does not catch everything.** Operation skips (non-scalar path params)
  and reserved-package collisions pass lint but break generation/`go build`. A clean lint is
  not proof it will generate and compile — run `speakeasy run` + `go build`.
- **Path parameters must be scalar.** An `anyOf`/object/array path param makes Speakeasy
  silently skip the whole operation (shown only as a "Critical warning" at generation time).
- **Reserved internal package names** for CLI targets include at least `usage`, `config`,
  `output`, `explorer`, `flagutil`. An OpenAPI tag matching one collides with the framework
  package (`usage redeclared` / `imported and not used`); fix with `x-speakeasy-group`.

## Repo layout

- `cmd/growthbook` — CLI entrypoint. `cmd/gendocs` — docs generator.
- `internal/sdk/` — generated SDK client. `internal/cli/<resource>/` — generated commands.
- `internal/` (config, output, explorer, flagutil, …) — Speakeasy framework support code.
- `.speakeasy/` — `workflow.yaml`, `gen.yaml`, `overlay.yaml` (the files you actually edit).
- `.goreleaser.yaml` — binary build/release config.
